root_text.iti101:2.1: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, appāni ceva sulabhāni ca, tāni ca anavajjāni.
root_text.iti101:2.3: Paṁsukūlaṁ, bhikkhave, cīvarānaṁ appañca sulabhañca, tañca anavajjaṁ.
root_text.iti101:2.4: Piṇḍiyālopo, bhikkhave, bhojanānaṁ appañca sulabhañca, tañca anavajjaṁ.
root_text.iti101:2.5: Rukkhamūlaṁ, bhikkhave, senāsanānaṁ appañca sulabhañca, tañca anavajjaṁ.
root_text.iti101:2.6: Pūtimuttaṁ, bhikkhave, bhesajjānaṁ appañca sulabhañca tañca anavajjaṁ.
root_text.iti101:2.7: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri appāni ceva sulabhāni ca, tāni ca anavajjāni.
root_text.iti101:2.8: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu appena ca tuṭṭho hoti sulabhena ca anavajjena ca, idamassāhaṁ aññataraṁ sāmaññaṅganti vadāmī”ti.
root_text.iti101:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti101:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti101:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti101:2.1: “Mendicants, these four trifles are easy to find and are blameless.
translation_text.iti101:2.5: Lodgings at the root of a tree …
translation_text.iti101:2.7: These four trifles are easy to find and are blameless.
translation_text.iti101:2.8: When a mendicant is content with trifles that are easy to find, they have one of the factors of the ascetic life, I say.”
translation_text.iti101:3.1: “When one is content with what’s blameless,
translation_text.iti101:4.1: These qualities are said to be
translation_text.iti101:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti101:2.8: anavajjena ca → etthantare pāṭho si, pts1ed, mr potthakesu ca 15A4:184 ca
